title: Collect logs and surface memory topics end to end

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
As an operator, I want Caducus to collect logs and surface semantic memory topics from them, so that I can see what patterns and anomalies matter now without manually reading raw log streams.

Feature: End-to-end log analysis into memory topics
  Scenario: Collect logs and analyze one group end to end
    Given a Caducus configuration that enables a log collector
    And the collector is configured to derive canonical events with id, timestamp, source, group_id, text, and metadata
    And the Caducus configuration contains a biblicus subtree for reinforcement memory settings
    When I run Caducus to collect events and analyze one group_id
    Then Caducus writes canonical event records for the collected logs
    And Caducus maps those events to Biblicus TimestampedText records
    And Caducus invokes Biblicus ReinforcementMemory.ingest and analyze for that group_id
    And Caducus shows structured memory topics including label, memory_tier, lifecycle_tier, member_count, and root_cause when present

  Scenario: Use collected logs as the source of truth for analysis
    Given canonical event records already exist for a group_id
    When I run Caducus analysis for that group_id
    Then Caducus reads the canonical events as the source of truth
    And Biblicus analysis state is treated as derived and reproducible

  Scenario: Preserve source metadata while clustering on text
    Given a collected log event contains additional source-specific fields
    When Caducus stores the canonical event and sends it to Biblicus
    Then Caducus uses event.text as the clustering text
    And Caducus preserves the additional source fields in metadata

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Executable Gherkin for demo-first vertical slice added in features/demo_vertical_slice.feature: (1) full demo run from local file -> canonical events -> Biblicus analyze -> CLI topics, (2) demo ingest only writes canonical events, (3) analyze reads from stored events and outputs stable topic shape.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Running the first real end-to-end HDFS demo on downloaded data. Goal: verify ingest, group discovery, and Biblicus analysis on a real component-derived group_id instead of only the tiny fixture/tests.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Real end-to-end demo executed successfully on downloaded HDFS_v1 data. Steps run: download 1000 rows to /tmp, ingest into Caducus storage, list discovered groups, analyze exact group_id 'hdfs-demo:dfs.DataNode'. Result: analysis completed with exit code 0 and produced topic output for 768 texts. One shell quoting pitfall was observed: component names containing '$' must be single-quoted in the shell.
